reason: Response A has a more analytical, structured quality that feels characteristic of Claude Sonnet — it builds a clear argument about branching patterns solving the same optimization problem, uses precise language without overreaching into ornate prose, and maintains a measured, explanatory tone throughout. Response B, while beautiful, leans into more literary flourishes ("characteristic thrift," "enormous patience," "strangely hopeful idea") and a more consciously poetic register that feels like an imitation attempting to sound elevated. A's closing paragraph ("Rivers do not plan their branching. They simply flow downhill. And yet...") has a directness and intellectual grounding more typical of Claude's actual style.
